business.headline: IBM shares plunge 26% as AI spending shift hits software businessThe company has been trying to reduce its reliance on the cyclical mainframe business by focusing on its software unit including its high-margin Red Hat business, which helps firms run applications across different cloud providers.

India tightens import rules, bans goods produced using forced labourThe decision is expected to strengthen India's import regulations and encourage businesses to pay closer attention to their global supply chains.

Dalal Street ends lower as Brent crude tops $86 amid fresh Middle East tensionsThe S&P BSE Sensex declined 570.10 points, or 0.73%, to settle at 77,046.30, while the NSE Nifty50 fell 171.10 points, or 0.71%, to close at 24,039.90.

Byju's founder can't return to Singapore without risking jail after court rulingSingapore's High Court rejected Byju Raveendran's plea to suspend a six-month contempt sentence. The ruling keeps the term in force and adds to his cross-border legal battles.

Tata is still India's No.1 brand. But there's a new entrant in the top 10Tata Group retained its place as India's most valuable brand for the 18th straight year, while Adani Group entered the top 10 for the first time. The Brand Finance India 100 2026 report also showed stronger gains for companies investing in AI, digital infrastructure and renewable energy.
Crude oil rises to a 1-month-high above $86: Will petrol and diesel prices increase too?At the time of writing, Brent Crude was trading at $86.07 per barrel, up around 3.33% during the day.

Have you recieved this 'total TDS' message? Here's what it meansThe Income Tax Department has started sending 'Total TDS' SMS alerts to taxpayers across the country. Why are you receiving this message, and what does it mean for your ITR? Have a look.

Inflation jumps from 2.74% to 4.38% in 6 months: Why did it rise so sharply?Food inflation climbed to 5.32% from 4.78% a month earlier. Since food carries the highest weight in the inflation basket, higher food prices had the biggest impact on overall inflation.

Why South Korea's KOSPI, world's best-performing stock market, is suddenly crashingSouth Korea's KOSPI has slipped into a bear market after a record AI-driven surge led by Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix. The reversal has exposed heavy retail leverage, foreign outflows and regulator concerns over single-stock products.

EPFO simplifies PF transfers: What the new rule means for job switchersThe change has been introduced after EPFO shifted to its Centralised IT Enabled Services (CITES) platform, which is designed to bring member records and services onto a single centralised system.

SBI Funds Management IPO GMP hints at 16% listing gains, but should you subscribe?SBI Funds Management is India's largest asset management company (AMC) and manages the SBI Mutual Fund. It is a joint venture between State Bank of India and French asset manager Amundi.

Trump wants a 20% Hormuz toll. What it could mean for India's fuel billIndia is in a better position to weather a disruption than it was a few months ago, but any prolonged instability around Hormuz could still push up oil prices, freight costs and inflation.

EPFO's plan to raise wage ceiling to Rs 25,000 put on hold. Here's whyAt present, the statutory wage ceiling for mandatory contributions under the Employees' Provident Fund (EPF) and Employees' Pension Scheme (EPS) is Rs 15,000 a month. The limit has remained unchanged since 2014.
Sensex, Nifty open lower as oil prices jump on fresh US-Iran tensionsThe S&P BSE Sensex fell 314.58 points to 77,301.82, while the NSE Nifty50 declined 85.45 points to 24,125.55 as of 9:35 am.

Nifty at 26,500? Goldman Sachs says these stocks, sectors could lead the rallyGoldman Sachs expects the benchmark index to reach 26,500, implying an upside of around 9.5% from Monday's closing level of 24,211. But more importantly for investors, it has identified the sectors and stocks it believes are best placed to benefit if the market recovery gathers pace.

SBI Funds Management IPO opens today: Should you subscribe? Check GMP, detailsThe SBI Funds Management IPO opens for subscription on July 14, 2026, and closes on July 16, 2026. The IPO is entirely an Offer for Sale (OFS), which means the company will not receive any money from the issue. Instead, the existing shareholders are selling part of their stake.

Why HDFC Bank reduced its workforce by over 3,300 despite growing businessThe annual report shows that HDFC Bank's total employee strength stood at 2,11,178 as of March 31, 2026, down by 3,343 employees from the previous year

Russia, Iran, North Korea moved $104 billion in crypto to bypass sanctions: ReportChainalysis says Russia, Iran and North Korea used cryptocurrencies to keep funds moving under sanctions in 2025. The report points to a fast-growing digital route for sanctions evasion, theft and restricted transactions.

BSNL's core services revenue rises 10% in Q1FY27 as govt pushes for its turnaroundBSNL's core services revenue rose to Rs 4,418 crore in Q1FY27 from 4,017 crore in the corresponding quarter last year, driven largely by growth in its Enterprise Business and Consumer Mobility segments.

India-US trade deal framework ready as talks progress amid tariff uncertaintyIn February, the two sides agreed to 18% tariffs on Indian goods in exchange for New Delhi lowering trade barriers and buying more American goods. But a final deal has been clouded by the U.S. Supreme Court ruling invalidating President Donald Trump's sweeping global tariffs.

entertainment.headline: Musafir Cafe trailer: Vikrant Massey navigates love, heartbreak and second chances The trailer for Musafir Cafe follows Chander as Sudha and Preeti reshape his life at different moments. The series presents love, heartbreak and ambition as forces that alter a person's path.

How Sonam Wangchuk made Aamir Khan's Rancho the hero every Indian needed Engineer-activist Sonam Wangchuk's life and ideas helped shape actor Aamir Khan's Rancho in 3 Idiots. Here is how that real-world foundation turned the film's protagonist into a survival guide for a burned-out generation.

Kishwer Merchant, Suyyash Rai mock Shivangi Joshi's Lock Upp breakdown Actors Kishwer Merchant and Suyyash Rai laughed at Shivangi Joshi's breakdown on Lock Upp: Sach Ya Sazaa in an Instagram video. The on-screen exchange and their off-screen reaction added to the wider online debate around the episode.

Kusha Kapila-Samay Raina row explained: The roast, fallout and the latest rumours Kusha Kapila has denied rumours of a reconciliation with Samay Raina after the two were seen at Jasleen Royal's birthday party. Her clarification has revived scrutiny of the roast fallout and the misinformation now circulating online.

Marvel's Avengers Doomsday gets early tickets sales; check trailer, runtime updates Marvel has revealed Avengers: Doomsday's runtime and launched premium tickets unusually early. The moves underline a major push around the film ahead of Comic-Con and its December 2026 release.

Kangana rejected Bollywood's boys' club. So why back Anandiben's kitchen politics? Kangana Ranaut once told women they were more than wives, mothers and daughters. Her latest endorsement of Anandiben Patel's remarks raises one uncomfortable question: What changed?

CM Vijay's Jana Nayagan to arrive in India a day before its overseas premiere Reports suggest Jana Nayagan may release in India on July 23, a day before its overseas debut. With censor clearance complete and bookings live abroad, the move could extend Vijay's opening weekend.

Yash's next with Sapta Saagaradaache Ello director? IMDb page sparks buzz A viral IMDb page has sparked speculation about Yash's next project after Toxic and Ramayana. The page links the actor's next project with Sapta Saagaradaache Ello director Hemanth M Rao.

Give it three hours: Sandeep Reddy Vanga urges audiences to watch Rao Bahadur Director Sandeep Reddy Vanga praised Telugu film Rao Bahadur on X and urged audiences to watch it. His endorsement comes as the film holds its theatrical run amid box office competition.

Samay Raina has taken court for a ride: Supreme Court fines comic Rs 3 lakh The Supreme Court has imposed a Rs 3 lakh fine on Samay Raina and four other comedians, saying he "took the court for a ride" in the disability jokes case. The top court said the comedian failed to honour assurances made during earlier hearings, accepting the Cure SMA Foundation's submissions before directing him to pay the penalty within two weeks.

Centre asks states to take action over Diljit Dosanjh's Satluj screenings: Sources The government is contemplating legal action over OTT release and public screenings of the Diljit Dosanjh-starrer Satluj, which lacked certification from the CBFC. Despite the film's removal from Zee5, community groups continue to screen it in gurdwaras, while a PIL seeks its restoration on the platform.

We need you: Naseeruddin Shah salutes Sonam Wangchuk, urges him to stop fasting Actor Naseeruddin Shah has urged Sonam Wangchuk to end his indefinite hunger strike, saying, "We need you" for the longer battle ahead. In a joint statement, he praised Wangchuk's courage while expressing concern over his deteriorating health.

After Ayodhya, Ranbir Kapoor buys 25.7 acres of land in Pune Actor Ranbir Kapoor has bought nearly 25.7 acres in Pune's Mulshi taluka for Rs 16.42 crore. The purchase follows his recent Ayodhya land deal as he prepares for Ramayana: Part 1.

Angelina Jolie, Brad Pitt's daughter Zahara seeks to drop 'Pitt' from surname Angelina Jolie and Brad Pitt's daughter, Zahara, has legally moved closer to dropping 'Pitt' from her surname. This follows a trend among their children, including Maddox and Shiloh, who have also distanced themselves from the 'Pitt' surname amid ongoing family tensions.

India will miss out on Christopher Nolan's true 70 mm vision for The Odyssey Ready to drop Rs 3,000 on a ticket? Here is why even India's IMAX screens will only show you a slice of director Christopher Nolan's towering vision for The Odyssey.

Supreme Court dismisses plea against renovation of Shah Rukh Khan's Mannat The Supreme Court dismissed a plea challenging the CRZ clearance for renovation work at Shah Rukh Khan's Mannat. The order leaves the NGT ruling untouched and keeps approval for the proposed two additional floors in place.

CM Vijay's childlike excitement over miniature EV car goes viral. Watch video At a Chennai Secretariat meeting, Chief Minister Joseph Vijay was seen fiddling with a miniature VinFast VF 8 electric SUV presented by Pham Sanh Chau. The candid exchange drew amused reactions online as fans said it revealed his playful side amid work and film commitments.

Ashutosh Gowariker on helming Temple Raiders: There was anger and compassion Ashutosh Gowariker said Temple Raiders was driven by anger, compassion and alarm over idol theft. He said the series aims to raise awareness and acknowledge efforts to recover India's stolen heritage.

India's Got Latent Season 2 more contained, says Kiku Sharda; out-of-line jokes dropped Actor-comedian Kiku Sharda opened up about the changes in India's Got Latent Season 2, saying comedian Samay Raina is now more careful with his humour. He also shared that some hilarious but 'out of line' moments were removed before the episode was released.

general news.headline: Trump drops 20% Hormuz toll plan, says Gulf investments will replace reimbursement feeTrump drops 20% Hormuz toll plan, says Gulf investments will replace reimbursement fee

Why POK is on the boil: India blames Pakistan's decades-long systemic exploitationThe government said India expects the global community to take note of the situation and hold Pakistan responsible for what he termed 'egregious abuses and misdeeds' in POK.

Indian-origin astronaut Anil Menon launched into space, begins 8-month missionNasa astronaut Anil Menon launched on an eight-month International Space Station mission to carry out medical and technology experiments.

Saudi-Houthi conflict hots up. Will Pakistan step in?Saudi Arabia and Yemen's Houthis have accused each other of fresh attacks, threatening to open a new front in the Middle East. The flare-up has renewed focus on whether Pakistan will be pressed to honour its defence pact with Riyadh.

India will miss out on Christopher Nolan's true 70 mm vision for The OdysseyReady to drop Rs 3,000 on a ticket? Here is why even India's IMAX screens will only show you a slice of director Christopher Nolan's towering vision for The Odyssey.

1st ODI Live: Rohit Sharma, Virat Kohli fall early in 259-run chaseEngland (ENG) vs India (IND), 1st ODI Live Score: Rohit Sharma and Virat Kohli have lost their wickets in 259-run chase at Birmingham. Sam Curran decieved Rohit with a slower one, while Kohli was beaten for pace by Archer.

India bans paraquat, weeds out biggest poison from its agricultural fieldsParaquat, the herbicide with no known antidote, survived years of scrutiny in India even as much of the world banned it over its toxicity. A draft notification by the Ministry of Agriculture on Tuesday imposed a nationwide prohibition on the chemical banned in over 70 countries.

Disabled man caught selling gutka dies in custody; DMK seeks answers from VijayThe custodial death of a physically disabled gutka sale accused has sparked outrage in Tamil Nadu, with the DMK demanding action against police personnel and answers from Chief Minister Vijay, who also holds the Home portfolio.

Should prenups become legal in India?When Celina Jaitly touched upon the idea of prenups, she wasn't just sharing personal advice, she was reopening a conversation about the importance of a financial document, one that India has probably long avoided.

Meet the IAS officer leading Maharashtra's toughest food safety crackdownMaharashtra's FDA has launched a statewide drive against milk adulteration under commissioner Tukaram Mundhe. The action has exposed synthetic milk units, disrupted supplies and sparked calls for compliance time.

ED arrests Vikas Garg in Mahadev betting app case, seizes Rs 940 crores in assetsThe arrest comes days after the ED attached assets worth Rs 940.77 crore belonging to Garg, his family members and companies allegedly owned or controlled by him.

Thailand keeps visa-free entry for Indians, but cuts stay to 30 daysThailand has dropped its plan to end visa-free entry for Indian tourists after seeing a decline in arrivals from India even before the proposal came into effect.
What happens if Donald Trump bombs Iran's Pickaxe Mountain?Donald Trump's threat to target Iran's Pickaxe Mountain has revived scrutiny of whether bunker-buster bombs can reach the fortified site.

Samay Raina has taken court for a ride: Supreme Court fines comic Rs 3 lakhThe Supreme Court has imposed a Rs 3 lakh fine on Samay Raina and four other comedians, saying he "took the court for a ride" in the disability jokes case. The top court said the comedian failed to honour assurances made during earlier hearings, accepting the Cure SMA Foundation's submissions before directing him to pay the penalty within two weeks.

Average car owner can't check mileage on their own: Nitin Gadkari amid E20 rowThe minister said car owners cannot accurately measure mileage themselves and should depend on readings taken at authorised dealers. His remarks came amid growing complaints over the government's ethanol blending mandate.

8 protesters killed in Pakistan Rangers' firing in PoK ahead of Muzaffarabad marchAhead of a planned mass march to Muzaffarabad, Pakistani authorities launched a crackdown across PoK, using tear gas and live fire to disperse protesters, with fresh firing also reported in Sudhanoti.

Kangana rejected Bollywood's boys' club. So why back Anandiben's kitchen politics?Kangana Ranaut once told women they were more than wives, mothers and daughters. Her latest endorsement of Anandiben Patel's remarks raises one uncomfortable question: What changed?

Remove your shades Balen Shah: Gen Z protesters return to Nepal streetsThe death of a 25-year-old ride-share driver who set himself on fire during a dispute with municipal police has sparked Gen Z-led protests across Nepal. Protesters and opposition lawmakers are demanding that Prime Minister Balen Shah, who rose to power on the youth-led wave, should resign.

2026 El Nino on path to become most powerful event in last 150 yearsClimate models indicate the 2026-27 El Nino could outstrip every event recorded since 1877. Scientists say its unusual speed and scale could disrupt weather worldwide, including India's monsoon.

The 136-year-old mosque that refused to move from Kolkata airportOver the decades, a mosque standing right within the premises of Kolkata airport has remained a point of contention. In fact, the Bankra mosque predates the airport. Now, the BJP government in Bengal has renewed efforts to relocate the mosque.

Pay Trump's Hormuz hafta or risk sleeping with the fishesAfter igniting the Middle East conflict and imperilling the Strait of Hormuz, US President Donald Trump said ships will be charged a fee for safe passage through the critical trade route. The proposal is not just brazen but downright fanciful.

Elephant returns 14 years later to kill 2 more members of same Nepali familyFour members of the same Nepal family were killed by a rogue elephant over 14 years, even after the family moved away hoping to escape the animal's deadly path.

Promises unfulfilled, Ryan ten Doeschate wants to quit India coaching jobRyan ten Doeschate is keen to leave the Indian cricket team after the England tour. According to sources, he is unhappy that he never received the specialist fielding coach's role he was promised.

Slovak leader's reaction after tasting Bihari Thekua gifted by PM Modi goes viralSlovak National Council Chairman Richard Raši received a gift of Bihari Thekua from Prime Minister Narendra Modi during his Slovakia visit. He then shared a video of him unboxing and tasting the sweet.

sports.headline: Another injury scare for India? Gurnoor Brar taken off in 1st ODI India suffered another injury scare during the first ODI against England after fast bowler Gurnoor Brar limped off the field while attempting to bowl his final over. The right-arm pacer had earlier sparked England's collapse with two quick wickets.

Watch: MS Dhoni melts hearts with sweet gesture for kid in stands at Edgbaston MS Dhoni stole the spotlight during the first ODI between India and England at Edgbaston, sharing a heartwarming moment with a young fan who offered him popcorn from the stands.

Promises unfulfilled, Ryan ten Doeschate wants to quit India coaching job Ryan ten Doeschate is keen to leave the Indian cricket team after the England tour. According to sources, he is unhappy that he never received the specialist fielding coach's role he was promised.

Shoulder injury continues to plague Sat-Chi as duo retire from Japan Open Satwiksairaj Rankireddy and Chirag Shetty retired after the opening game of their Japan Open opener as Rankireddy's shoulder injury flared up again. The setback will make them skip the China tournament, while PV Sindhu gave India brighter news by moving into the second round.

Kuldeep Yadav pays the price for Gambhir's all-rounder obsession, again India left out Kuldeep Yadav for the first ODI against England and packed the XI with three all-rounders on Tuesday. Abhishek Nayar said the call sacrificed a wicket-taking middle-overs option for batting depth.

Erling Haaland returns from the US with a raccoon, breaks the internet Despite Norway's heartbreaking World Cup exit, Erling Haaland has stolen the global spotlight by returning home with a bizarre, whiskey-clutching taxidermied raccoon. Now, the internet is completely obsessed with his viral new Texas travel companion.

1st ODI Live: Rohit Sharma, Virat Kohli fall early in 259-run chase England (ENG) vs India (IND), 1st ODI Live Score: Rohit Sharma and Virat Kohli have lost their wickets in 259-run chase at Birmingham. Sam Curran decieved Rohit with a slower one, while Kohli was beaten for pace by Archer.

Friction between Gambhir and Agarkar? India urged to clear confusion after UK disaster Dinesh Karthik said Gautam Gambhir and Ajit Agarkar appear out of sync after India's T20 defeats in Ireland and England. India lost T20I series in England and Ireland without even winning a game.

PV Sindhu dominates Wong Ling Ching to seal Japan Open Round 2 berth PV Sindhu made a dominant start to her Japan Open Super 750 campaign, defeating Malaysia's Wong Ling Ching 21-14, 21-11 to advance to the women's singles second round on Tuesday.

Messi's Argentina face England next, but football can't forget the Hand of God FIFA World Cup 2026: As England and Argentina prepare to renew one of football's fiercest rivalries, the shadow of Diego Maradona's Hand of God still looms large. Technology may have transformed the game, but some World Cup memories simply refuse to fade.

India honour Heather Knight, Tammy Beaumont as England legends bid farewell India paid an emotional tribute to retiring England stars Heather Knight and Tammy Beaumont after sealing a historic 270-run win in the first-ever women's Test at Lord's, with the duo receiving guard of honours and special jerseys from the visitors.

Can Afghan women dream? ICC targets 2030 qualification pathway for refuge teamThe ICC has set a target of integrating Afghan refugee women cricketers into qualification events by 2030, offering fresh hope to players forced into exile after the Taliban takeover in 2021.

Mbappe cleared fit as France coach plays mind games before semifinal vs SpainFIFA World Cup: Kylian Mbappe has been declared fully fit to lead France, but manager Didier Deschamps wasted no time playing mind games by labeling Spain the clear favourites ahead of their blockbuster semifinal in Dallas.

Coaching England, Brendon McCullum roots for Virat Kohli in ODI series vs IndiaEngland white-ball coach Brendon McCullum lavished praise on Virat Kohli ahead of the ODI series against India, calling him one of cricket's all-time greats and hoping the former India captain enjoys a successful campaign.

Draped in diamonds, birthday boy Yamal delivers strong message against racismFIFA World Cup 2026: Sporting a stunning self-gifted diamond necklace on his 19th birthday, Spain prodigy Lamine Yamal lit up Dallas with a powerful message of integration and unity ahead of a highly anticipated clash against France.

France vs Spain: Mbappe and Yamal headline World Cup semi-final showdownFIFA World Cup 2026: France and Spain meet in the World Cup semi-final in Dallas after contrasting runs to the last four. The tie pits Mbappe's direct attacking threat against Spain's control, with a place in the final at stake.

Harry Kane blames English mentality for rumours of rift before Argentina semifinalFIFA World Cup: Harry Kane has dismissed suggestions of a rift between Thomas Tuchel and the England squad following Jude Bellingham's emotional post-match remarks after the quarter-final win over Norway, insisting the camp remains united as focus shifts to the World Cup semi-final against Argentina.

PV Sindhu, Lakshya Sen and Sat-Chi begin tune-up for World ChampionshipsPV Sindhu and Sat-Chi lead India's Japan Open campaign in Tokyo. The Super 750 offers World Championships preparation as Sat-Chi return from injury and India chase a first elusive title.

1st ODI preview: India rely on comeback men Rohit, Virat to lift dampened spiritsEngland vs India, 1st ODI preview: India will look to seal their first win on the tour after a disastrous T20I series in England. The return of Virat Kohli, Rohit Sharma and Jasprit Bumrah adds firepower to the side, led by Shubman Gill.

Why the football world has turned against ArgentinaFIFA World Cup 2026: Argentina's rise from Messi's tragic nearly-men to world champions has changed how football fans view them. Their success now attracts suspicion, backlash and scrutiny that often follows title-winning sides.

Arshad Nadeem confirms participation in CWG 2026, set to face Neeraj ChopraThe Neeraj Chopra-Arshad Nadeem rivalry is set for another chapter after the Pakistan star confirmed his participation in the Commonwealth Games 2026. Nadeem will defend the gold medal he won in Birmingham, while Chopra returns to the Games after missing the previous edition through injury.

RCB coach Andy Flower will not leave IPL to join England Test team: KarthikRCB head coach Andy Flower is one of the early favourites to become the head coach of the England Test team. However, former India cricketer, Dinesh Karthik believes that there is one small problem which would stop the coach from switching sides to England.

Brendon McCullum breaks silence after sacking from England Test coach roleBrendon McCullum said he was disappointed after losing the England Test job and apologised to supporters. He said that Bazball changed the side's identity, but failures in the Ashes and against India proved decisive.

Harmanpreet calls for more women's Test matches after historic win at Lord'sIndia captain Harmanpreet Kaur has called for more women's Test matches after leading her side to a historic 270-run victory over England at Lord's. Speaking after India's massive win, she hoped that the team gets "ten more Test matches" to continue proving themselves in the longest format.

Loved Sooryavanshi's Wimbledon look? Decoding his Centre Court style gameLiked Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's sharp look at the All England Club? From his structured black suit and striped club tie to those sleek leather loafers, we decode the teenage batting prodigy's effortless, last-minute Wimbledon style game.

Retirement far off, Kohli helping India plan for ODI World Cup 2027India captain Shubman Gill has revealed that former India captain Virat Kohli is heavily involved in the planning of ODI World Cup 2027. Gill also answered queries about Rohit Sharma and his place in the team going into the marquee tournament.

History at Home of Cricket: India win first-ever women's Test at Lord'sIt took more than 140 years for Lord's to host its first-ever women's Test. India made the wait worthwhile, hammering England by 270 runs. Yastika Bhatia's century, Smriti Mandhana's twin fifties and Kranti Gaud's five-wicket haul powered a landmark victory at the Home of Cricket.

Sachin drops in at Lord's, leads pep talk as India women chase historic winSachin Tendulkar paid a surprise visit to the Indian women's team at Lord's ahead of the fourth day of the historic one-off Test against England. He was seen leading the talk to the team as Harmanpreet Kaur's side closed in on a famous victory.

Day 4 Highlights: India win by 270 runs, win first-ever women's Test at Lord'sEngland (ENG) vs India (IND) Day 4 Highlights, women's one-off Test: Harmanpreet Kaur's India win the first ever women's Test match at Lord's. India beat England by a massive margin of 270 runs.

After dashing Wimbledon appearance, Sanju Samson reveals Novak Djokovic inspirationAt Wimbledon, Sanju Samson said Novak Djokovic's discipline and demeanour remain a benchmark for athletes. Yuvraj Singh backed that view, highlighting Djokovic's self-belief, longevity and influence on younger players across the globe.

Australian cricket rocked by cheating scandal days after T20 Women's World Cup winAustralia women's cricket has been engulfed in controversy after Ashleigh Gardner's estranged wife, Monica Wright, publicly accused the all-rounder of having an affair with teammate Georgia Voll. Here's everything we know.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
